name: Real-Time Voice Agent V2
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure AI Voice Live
services.purpose: Bidirectional voice streaming — sub-200ms end-to-end latency, real-time speech-to-speech pipeline, barge-in detection for natural interruptions, voice activity detection, echo cancellation, noise suppression, multi-language real-time switching

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o Realtime for conversational intelligence — function calling for MCP tool invocation, multi-turn dialogue management, context-aware responses, personality and tone control, dynamic intent classification, knowledge base grounding

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Voice agent runtime — WebSocket session management, MCP tool server hosting, avatar rendering pipeline, session state machine, voice activity detection processing, multi-agent routing, real-time audio mixing

prod.note: 4 vCPU, 8GB, min 3 replicas, GPU for avatar

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: MCP tool execution — external API integrations (CRM, calendar, payments, booking), database lookups, knowledge base retrieval, webhook processing, session event triggers, post-call summarization and analytics

enterprise.note: 8 vCPU, dedicated compute, always-ready for low latency

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Session and conversation state — active session context, conversation history, user profiles, tool execution results, call recordings metadata, analytics aggregates, agent configuration, knowledge base cache

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Voice service credentials, OpenAI API keys, MCP tool secrets, CRM/payment integration tokens, session encryption keys, avatar rendering certificates

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Voice agent KPIs — end-to-end latency (target <200ms), barge-in success rate, tool invocation latency, conversation completion rate, user satisfaction score, avatar rendering FPS, concurrent session metrics

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for simple tool dispatch (calendar lookup, FAQ retrieval) and reserve GPT-4o-realtime for complex multi-turn reasoning, negotiation, and emotionally sensitive conversations — saving 60% on AI costs for routine calls
Implement session pooling in Container Apps — pre-warm WebSocket connections and reuse session infrastructure across sequential calls; cold-starting a full voice session adds 2-3 seconds of latency

Cache frequently accessed knowledge base responses in Cosmos DB with 1-hour TTL — common questions (business hours, pricing, return policy) don't need real-time AI inference; pre-computed responses reduce latency to <50ms

Right-size avatar rendering — use lightweight 2D avatar for voice-only calls and reserve GPU-accelerated 3D avatar rendering for video-enabled sessions; most voice agent interactions don't need visual avatars

Use Functions consumption plan for post-call analytics (summarization, sentiment analysis, CRM updates) — these are not latency-sensitive; batch processing after call completion saves 80% vs. premium plan
Implement intelligent silence detection — during long user pauses, reduce GPU and inference resource allocation; resume full resources when voice activity resumes, saving 30% on compute costs
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
